# hotato

> The open (MIT), offline, zero-install turn-taking eval for voice agents. Does
> your agent drop the turn, or hog it? It scores turn-taking from a call
> recording and points each failure at a concrete fix. Built to be called by an
> AI agent mid-task, as a CLI or a one-tool MCP server. No account, no API key,
> no cloud, no audio egress.

## Run it (zero-install)

- Score the bundled labelled battery: `uvx hotato run --suite barge-in`
- Score one recording (two-channel WAV, caller and agent on separate channels):
  `uvx hotato run --stereo call.wav --stack livekit --format json`
- As a one-tool MCP server (stdio): `uvx --from "hotato[mcp]" hotato-mcp`
  exposes exactly one tool, `voice_eval_run`, returning the identical JSON envelope.

## What it returns

A single JSON envelope (schema: `hotato/schema/envelope.v1.json`,
`schema_version` "1", additive-only). Per event, three objective timing signals:
`did_yield`, `seconds_to_yield`, `talk_over_sec`, plus a `signals` bus
(`barge_in`, `latency`) and a `verdict`. Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = a
regression, 2 = usage/IO error. Every failing event carries a `fix`:

- `fix_class: "config"` — a concrete knob for your stack (livekit|pipecat|vapi|
  generic) with the direction and the honest trade-off. No upsell.
- `fix_class: "engagement-control"` — the failure is a discrimination problem a
  single sensitivity dial cannot solve; points high-level (no numbers, no
  vendor) at the KIND of fix it needs — a learned engagement-control /
  addressee-detection layer, an open research problem, not a config knob or a
  product you can buy.

## Honest limits (read before trusting a number)

- No accuracy percentage anywhere. These are reproducible timing measurements
  with an exposed method and an explicit ceiling, not judgements of a detector's
  internal quality. Every threshold is a documented, overridable parameter and
  every frame is inspectable via `--dump-frames`.
- Energy is not intent: no speaker identification, no diarization, no
  transcription, no emotion detection.
- Best input is a two-channel recording; mono is degraded and requires an onset
  label. Bundled fixtures are synthetic — a floor and a regression guard, not
  production audio. Bring your own labelled calls for real validity.

## Links

- README.md — quickstart and the honest ceiling.
- METHODOLOGY.md — exactly how every number is computed.
- CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/CORPUS-GOVERNANCE.md — contribute real labelled fixtures.
- LICENSE — MIT. The open core is never relicensed.
